Seattle Internship description ISB is a nonprofit research organization where scientists collaborate on cross-disciplinary biomedical research disciplines. Using Python, I created programs that read mass spectra, compute metrics of each pectrum, select certain mass spectra for further analysis, and write new data files with the selected information. locations: South Lake Union Internship description The Mulligan/Hwang lab focuses on researching exosomes and their application to lung biology. Harborview Medical Center Internship description There are many different departments where you can volunteer at Harborview Medical Center. Working at the Information Desk department means knowing the hospital layout, finding patients appointments, and solving various patient related problems. At the Family Medicine clinic, the tasks are mostly comprised of clerical work and stocking provider rooms with medical equipment. Unique experiences Working in a Level 1 Trauma Center Talking with patients and gaining customer service experience (at the information desk) Learning the inner workings of a clinic Meeting a lot of physicians, pharmacists, PA's, MA's etc.

Elegans model. As a high school intern, I primarily recorded, processed, and analyzed data, and presented such data in the form of graphs and statistics to the mentor and a team of interns. Unique experiences Saw how certain drugs and genes could be used to slow the progression of neurodegenerative diseases, such as with the Alzheimeds Disease gene. Toured the UW labs and Health Sciences building. In addition to working with the mentor, also had opportunities to work with current undergraduate students for further mentorship. This allowed for learning about the internship, experiences as a biology student at the LIV and delving deeper into the biological processes behind these experiments.

Seattle Internship description attle Children's houses a wide variety of labs ranging from molecular biology to tech research. I work in the attle Children's Innovative Technologies Lab, which researches ways to apply tech in autism research. As an intern, I cleaned and entered data from past studies, wrote literature reviews and compiled publications with my mentor, and worked on side projects relevant to behavioral and tech research. Seattle Children's typically only hires UW students, so as a high schooler I never had access to the system or PHI but was still able to help in other ways. Unique experiences Writing a literature review on applications of eye tracking in autism research Helping to research and compile sources for a book on eye tracking as a potential psychiatric biomarker Working on Al generated images for an equity in healthcare careers comic book surprised you most? I was surprised by the amount of coordination and time that goes into a successful study, we were still processing data from studies completed 8 years ago and working to publish any findings. t is your greatest takeaway(s) from the internship experience?

Seattle (307 Westlake AVE) Internship description At the Vaughan Lab in the Seattle Children's Research Institute, Center for Global Infectious Disease, the primary focus is research into malaria through the use genetic recombination with the ultimate goa of alleviating the suffering of those infected. My role was largely that of a research assistant, helping the research scientists with their experiments, recording data, and creating data tables to be published. My work primarily dealt with the P. falciparum strand, genetic crosses of the lab-strain with wild-strains from across Africa (Sudan, Mali, and Malawi), end developing a vaccine that is more representative of malaria in the field. By measuring and comparing the effectiveness of these hybrid crosses at infecting the liver, we sought to determine which hybrid is most suitable and effective for vaccine development. Unique experiences Hear from scientists that speak at global conferences. Work with human malaria and humanized mouse livers through laboratory procedures such as PCR, IFA, microscopy etc. Analyze different research papers and learn more about publication through first-hand experience.
summary: The Tang Lab focuses on research into the brain and determining where the conscious is located in the brain. My mentors, Lucia Pazzagli and Ashley Vaughan, are using genetic recombination to create a Pan-African strain of malaria, which will make future vaccine research more accurate and efficient....

Downtown Seattle Internship description Seattle Litigation Group focuses on civil litigation, employment and administrative law and represents clients in Washington State. During my time at Seattle Litigation Group, I mostly followed a paralegal and was gradually introduced to legal processes and concepts over time. I often scheduled client consultations, drafted/sent out letters, and assisted in any general task that the firm had for me. Unique experiences Connecting with real clients on the phone Walking down to the physical location of King County Bar Association and meeting with professionals Getting basic office knowledge that I didn't know I needed Realizing that I had been growing my knowledge of the legal field over time and practice

Seattle Internship description I assist a paralegal at a law firm in downtown Seattle that focuses on employment, administrative, and school law! I help with much of what a paralegal does: handling real cases, interacting with clients via mail, and Miti ng/editing media for the firm. This internship will often allow you to selecttasks based on our interests. For example, If you're tired of writing, you could be given photo or video editing projects! Unique experiences Interacting with lots of cases evetyweek: intakes, scanning, emails, etc. Researching the RCW (Revised Code of Washington) • Designing holiday cards for the office to send out Editing actual YouTube videos for the firm and uploading them •
summary: During my internship I learned thoroughly about SLG's online management system Clio. One of my tasks was to read and summarize new client intake responses for attorneys to read. A lot of my work involved scanning documents, as well as summarizing intakes (which are a potential client's answers to the firm's questions which asks them to describe their situation). There was also organizational work on Clio, which is what the firm uses to keep track of their matters. I also did some front desk work with taking calls...

Bellevue City Hall Internship description The Bellevue Department of Transportation is what makes our city move. Bus lines, light rail, and road repairs may seem like very/' mundane things, but their impact over time is immeasurable. The simple addition of having a light rail stop could mean the difference between a neighborhood turning •nto a bustling suburb or becoming a ghost town. Transportation decisions have a huge impact an whether a community thrives or dies. Furthermore, it is extremely difficult to know where, when, and how transportation projects are needed, resulting in many communities being neglectedv My internship is to help ensure that every community thrives, through increasing pu blic involvement with discriminated people. One of the main ways the transportation department plans its projects is through feedback from the public. But many people cannot give feedback, due to language barriers, time restrictions. distrust in government. and countless other reasons. Without feedback from those people, their communities are left behind. By researching newways to reach out to those discriminated groups, transportation projects can be distributed more equitably. Unique experiences I had many unique experiences by meeting other people. For example, meeting councilman Conrad Lee. I also got to know the other kids who intern for Kristi, who are from Big Picture. They are all great people who can provide you with even more connections.

This entailed using digital tools to survey planning areas, collect and aggregate data, and present them in an informative and useful manner. The City of Bellevue's Transportation...

locations: Interlake High School Internship description Being a teaching assistant for biology entails setting up labs and organizing lab equipment, working closely with the biology teachers, organizing and creating lesson plans, managing and overseeing classes, grading, making copies of classroom materiels necessary for certain activities, etc. The teachers really appreciate the help, as there is a lot of work that they do behind the scenes that we don't know about. The hours depend on your mentor, but usually it includes 6th and 7th period throughout the week where you will be tasked with various items. Although it doesn't seem important* it is a great help to the teachers and it gets you experience with materials end procedures you may need for the future. It also puts a different perspective an learning which can be applicable to your own work. Unique experiences Learning how to utilize the copy machine Being able to evaluate student work Basic biology knowledge to setup labs and procedures Learning how to create assignments for students to optimize their learning

locations: Seattle Internship description Robodub Inc. is a Seattle based drone company that specializes in self-reconfiguring quadcopters. Robodub eveloped Morphing technology that allows quadcopters and multi-rotor drones to reconfigure their rotor ositions mid-flight, making drones more efficient, safe, and load adaptable. With an emphasis on optimized erformance, Robodub has executed contracts with the US Navy and Army. During my time at Robodub, I esearched the adaptations and physics behind sliding-rotor Plus Configuration quadcopters. Additionally, I sed the Scilab software for graphing and simulations. Unique experiences Basics regarding aerodynamics and physics of drones and quadcopters Vast experimentation with designs and simulations through Scilab. Graphing and tracking exclusive to a certain configuration of quadcopter. Unique experience with hardware with the ability to build and fly a drone from scratch, with support from industry professionals.

University of Washington Internship description The Sensors, Energy, and Automation Laboratory (SEAL) at the University of Washington works in the areas of sensors, energy, and automation. Findings from research are applied to solving problems in medicine, avionics, wearable tech, defense, energy, and sustainability. I was part of the Industrial Assessment Center team, which is focused on the development oftechnical solutions for energy efficiency and sustainability, and applies knowledge toward helping businesses be more energy efficient. As a high school interny I helped other members who performed on site assessments by writing templates, research papers, and emailing businesses. As you can tell from my picture, I didn't learn any photography! Unique experiences Opportunity to help well known companies become more energy efficient Learn the structure of a UW lab Research and apply knowledge about energy Write papers and do individual research

locations: Online/8eIlevue City Hall Internship description My internship was about transportation planning within Bellevue. My mentor handled a variety of things (like mapping, streets, and transit) and I was assigned to sidewalk construction. While sidewalks seem simple, the process is a bit bureaucratic. I outreached to residents about the city's safety initiatives, gathered their requests for sidewalks in their neighborhoods, evaluated their effectiveness and cost using city databases, and presented my findings to the transportation committee who makes the decisions. I also occasionally worked on other projects in the transportation and other departments. Unique experiences Learning how to use the city's transportation databases, like Mapshot and TIMS Seeing how city initiatives and policies are implemented and how residents can work with the government to get stuff built in their neighborhoods Overall getting experience in how city governments work

locations: University of Washington Internship description Kaeberlein Lab, part of the UW Laboratory Medicine and Pathology Department, focuses on aging and aging- related diseases. The lab focuses on many model organisms to study diseases that range from Parkinson's to metabolic dysfunction. My group focused on Parkinson's and Alzheimer's and their respective models in C. elegans. Starting at Kaeberlein did require basic knowledge in biology, but will allow interns to grow in a specialty, in my case being neurodegenerative diseases. Unique experiences • Planning, executing, and presenting original scientific designs • Data analysis of drug tests on model organisms • Growing skills in conducting scientific literary research

University of Washington Internship description Dr. Brandon Berry explores the role of mitochondrial dysfunction in aging, using C. elegans as a model organism. My time with Dr. Berry provided me with hands-on experience in the pursuit of scientific research. Outies included wet-lab procedures with direct supervision, image analysis with ImageJ, worm maintenance, and scientific literature discussion. There are also opportunities to learn how different equipment is used such as the Worm80t and different types of microscopes. The internship is very adaptable depending on whatJs needed and everyone's availabilityv Unique experiences While I was there, my mentor submitted a paper to a very prestigious journal. The article was peer reviewed, and we discussed the comments and how to implement the edits. It was very interesting to see the real-time process of paper submission and review. gat to run my own experiments involving the movement of worms in a liquid medium and wrote a short report on the results.

locations: Kirkland, Washington Internship description The Law Office of Jenny Cochrane is a general practice firm that handles a variety of legal matters. My experience as an intern consisted mainly of client outreach and assisting the paralegals and Ms. Cochrane wth paperwork for different cases. The tasks changed daily based on what was needed for the casesv The day-to-day experience varied from scanning documents for clients' case files, reaching out to potential new clients, typing case notes, communicating with opposing counsel, creating exhibit binders for trials etc. There are also many organizational tasks to be done around the office such as organizing the client contact information and the client hard files. Unique experiences Communicating with first-time clients and taking notes/summaries of their cases. Creating exhibit binders for trials. Listening in on how a virtual trial operates (could shift to in-person trials). Processing and recording payments for clients.

locations: Bellevue Internship description ELAP works to provide legal services to Iow-income residents of king countyv I helped ELAP work towards this goal by engaging in administrative tasks and general employee assistance These tasks ranged from messaging and calling clients to creating and editing various files used to secure and track funding, as well as office management. However, some of the other interns at ELAP differed from my experience, where they specialized and worked more with certain employees, while I handled work from a variety of sources. Unique experiences A unique experience I got out of this internship was being able to self-advocate and stay focused on work. Oue to certain time periods needing to be remote, I had to force myselfto communicate with the employees and keep myself focused, something that was quite different from remote schooling.

Bellevue Internship description ELAP is a pro or Iow bono law firm that offers legal assistance to those who otherwise could not afford it. Some of its services include: educating communities about their legal rights, empowering domestic violence survivors and their families to stay safe, supporting victims of crime as they navigate the legal system, removing legal barriers that impact people's health, protecting people facing eviction so they can stay in their homes, giving advice ta people who are being harassed by debt collection agencies, and advocating for immigrants, regardless of their citizenship status. Unique experiences Chance to work with clients and get involved in cases Learn more about how an office works

locations: University of Washington Seattle Internship description Dr. Gelb is one of the leading experts in newborn screening. Newborn screening refers the tests done on a baby when thev are first born ta make sure they are healthy. Right now, the Gelb Lab is largely focused an esting end research for genetic disease called metachromatic leukodystrophy IMI-DI. Interning for Or. Gelb, I worked on two different projects, Forthe first half of the year, I worked on excel to organize data from one Or. Gelb's studies into a form that could be uploaded to ClinVar ta be publicly available for doctors ta use. Forthe second half of the year, I assisted one of Or, Gelb's graduate students while he was doing a wet lab} nd I gatto prepare samples through pipetting, centrifuges, incubators, and more. Unique experiences I got to attend a biochemistry conference with graduate students and professors, making contacts in the industry end learning about current biochemistry studies. Experience in a wet lab with real samples. • I felt like the work I helped with had a direct impact on the lives of others, which was incredibly motivating. at surprised you most? I was surprised by how much I learned about the professional experience of working in biochemistry end biology research. I was also surprised by how many contacts I made at a biochemistry conference. The nference was a surreal experience and felt like a complete sample of professional life. at is your greatest takeaway(s) from the internship experience? I learned a ton about how real biology and biochemistry labs operate. Doing work with a huge amount of ata, I got to teach myself things about excel I never knew and will definitely use in the future.

organization: Elementary Particles Experiment Machine Learning Study Group
locations: UW Internship description Professor Shih-Chieh Hsu leads many PhD and post-grad students in research in the fields of high energy physics and machine learning. Members of the EPE ML study group study the fundamentals of python programming and machine learning through tutorials designed by EPE as well as UW and NTHU courses. The group meets weekly to share progress and discuss coursework, and members of the group are expected to attend weekly EPE meetings to learn about the different research projects end eventually implement the ML knowledge by shadowing/participating in one of the research projects lead by PhO/postgrad students and supervised by Professor Hsu. Unique experiences Learning fundamentals in high energy physics end machine learning, basics of programming„ and experience in physics research. Most of the work is independent and remote. A lot of self-motivation is needed to get through the coursework and materials. Professor Hsu is super passionate about his research!!

UW Internship description Professor Shih-Chieh Hsu leads many PhD and postgrad students in research in the applications of machine learning to high energy physics. Interns of the EPE ML Study Group first learn the fundamentals of Python programming, Pytorch, and machine learning through the UW PHYS 427 labs designed by EPE. They are expected to collaborate and share progress during weekly meetings with Professor Hsu. Additionally, interns are expected to learn about the different research projects of the EPE group and eventually join one of the projects to apply ML knowledge. They will then work closely with the PhD/postgrad student leading the project and share findings with Professor Hsu and fellow interns in weekly meetings. Unique experiences • Learning about current research in high energy physics and machine learning • So many different projects to choose from —calorimetry clustering, gravitational waves, etc. o Professor Hsu wants you to choose a project you're truly passionate in, so you can always switch if something else interests you • Mostly remote but weekly meetings are at UW surprised you most? I went into this internship with no ML knowledge and limited coding experience but became proficient enough to understand ongoing research • Professor Hsu is very open to students who have limited experience in ML or particle physics. He gives you the necessary resources and time to ensure you have enough knowledge before joining a research project • The research Professor Hsu is supervising is all really interesting, and it got me inspired to pursue ML is your greatest takeaway(s) from the intemship experience? Research takes a lot of self-motivation and rigor but as long as you're interested, it is exciting and rewarding. our mentor is there to support you, so never be afraid to ask questions or for help.

locations: Seattle University Physics Department/Remote Internship description This internship was similar to the sort of research you would do in a semester/year-long independent research class you would take in college. I worked with Dr. aoness to learn about the equations of state, exoplanets, and how data is collected for objects far off in space in my first couple months of the internship. After my initial few months, I was mostly reading a lot of papers on thermodynamics of Earth's core and other planets. I also learned to code in python and compiled data from various papers to create graphs on the Pressure-Temperature-volume relationships on certain compounds found on exoplanets. After transitioning to remote half-wav through the year, I was still able continue with the internship as I was before, This internship was a lot of reading published geophysics papers and coding. Unique experiences I enjoyed getting to work with a physics/chem[stry student at Seattle University. The internship could be what you wanted it to be, and it was very independent. If I wanted to go deeper into a certain topic, could do so. If I wanted to learn more about an overarching theme, I could do so as welln I did a "unit" on the exoplanet WASP-12b, and I was also able to research and read into the factors that indicate the possibility of life.

organization: University of Washington Industrial Assessment Center
locations: University of Washington/Online remote Internship description ponsored by the US Department of Energy, the Industrial Assessment Center Program provides no-cost assessments to manufacturing companies nationwide, recommending budget-friendly solutions to prepare hem to lead the global clean energy transition. At the University of Washington's local IAC, we provide such ervices for companies in Washington State as our contribution to this great endeavor. During my time at IAC, I learned and worked with UW students and professors to sort and analyze assessment data, research ways o improve energy efficiency, and compile detailed reports for our client businesses. With only one weekly meeting, the schedule is very relaxing, and I often find myself losing track of time in my work! Unique experiences Researching advanced ways to save energy on a mass scale Writing professional and detailed assessment reports Touring industrial facilities with industry professionals surprised you most? I was surprised by the sheer efficiency of the IAC team in conducting facility tours, analyzing data, and presenting recommendations. The whole process is very streamlined and every recommendation is saved as a template to be reused for similar situations. is your greatest takeaway(s) from the intemship experience?

locations: Hans Rosling Center for Population Health Internship description Under Dr. Yanfang Su, we worked on several research projects mostly •nvolving health policy and data analysis. I primarily conducted literature reviews, wrote abstracts and introductions, and did final reviews. Unique experiences Go through the research process— learn new frameworks, literature reviews, grant applications, writing abstracts, manuscript reviews Small research paper presentations Network with heelth professionals at local conferences and talks at surprised you most? I was surprised to see how interdisciplinary global health was. People from all backgrounds including policymaking, data science, healthcare, and much more work together to solve problems that previously I didn't know involved this many people. I appreciate how difficult it is o tackle the world's health problems — sa many roadblocks and walls between people need to be crossed. at is your greatest takeaway(s) from the internship experience? I understand the world a little better because ofthis internship, Sa manyfactors play into health, nd many of them seem counterintuitive. Additionally, I frequently worked on tasks I had little to no experience with. I learned how ta figure out these tasks quickly by searching for appropriate papers or documentation. For instance, I found ways to use Python (familiar) instead of R (the ndard but unfamiliar) to do data analysis end visualization.

locations: Opera Center on the Seattle Center Campus Internship description e Seattle Opera is one of the world's leading Opera companies, presenting a variety of mainstage Operas ach year at McCaw Hall on top of their robust community engagement programs. During my time at Seattle Opera, I worked primarily with the Programs and Partnerships (P+P) Departments, which organizes all of the attle Opera's outreach programs and events. Unique experiences Attended Seattle Opera All-Staff, Equity Team and Accessibility Committee meetings Reached out to schools and prepared scores, librettos and costumes for Seattle Opera School Tour Assisted at Youth Opera Project and Teen Vocal Studio rehearsals Built Balloon arches and cut out Valkyrie wings for Seattle Opera Events at surprised you most? I was most surprised at how much my mentor encouraged me to learn about how a large-scale arts non profit organization works, not just work on departmental projects. Everyone was always super open to me itting in on meetings and interacting with other departments. at is your greatest takeaway(s) from the intemship experience? I found it incredibly inspiring to intern in an arts organization that really valued working towards Opera and he arts more accessible to others. It was not only inspiring to help organize events like the Seattle Opera's Irst relaxed performance, but also see some of the higherups like our Artistic Director attend and be invested in the work of the Equity Team.

organization: University of Washington Electrical and Computer Engineering Department
locations: Paul G Allen Center for CSE Internship description The EMiT lab at the University of Washington works on developing emerging photonic technology for biological applications. The project I was assigned to was oriented toward using laser speckle contrast imaging (LSCI) to image blood perfusion in burn injuries. I edited code for image processing, designed microcontroller systems to capture test images, and created documentation and project presentations for other members in the lab. Unique experiences • Learn about the physics behind LSCI. Integrate computer science, engineering, and a bit of biology into a single project. Broad exposure to engineering disciplines. • Learn how to use popular Python modules at surprised you most? I was surprised at how much time was spent not on writing code, but on testing and debugging it. A big aspect of engineering is testing and revising, and I learned how to leverage online resources to navigate unfamiliar technology. t is your greatest takeaway(s) from the intemship experience? My biggest takeaway is to pay attention to detail. The smallest detail can have large impacts on an engineering project. A single chip with subpar writing speed can become a big headache to resolve later on.

University of Washington Seattle Internship description I worked with a professor from the University of Washington's Electrical and Computer Engineering Department throughout the summer and the school year. As a part of the internship, I took a summer quarter quantum mechanics class (graduate student level). Using this knowledge, I worked with my professor to write and edit the math of the textbook that he plans to publish in the near future. During the school year, I authored a solution manual independently. Unique experiences Basic to intermediate quantum physics/computing understanding Taking an actual course at UW with graduate students (for free!) Having the trust and guidance of an expert in the field, who provided great support systems for growth and learning

summary: In my internship, I worked on analyzing the impact of introducing Regional Air Mobility (eVTOL aircraft) in the West Virginia region. I learned about how aircraft are designed based on demand and network data. Then, I worked on collecting data for my specific case study, which focuses on designing a RAM aircraft to travel between Blacksburg, Virginia and Washington, DC. At the end of the year, I worked on developing a mode choice model to predict aircraft characteristics.
activities: • Learning about the steps in the process of legacy aircraft design • Conducting data analysis on West Virginia's transportation network

organization: Elementary Particle Experiment Machine Learning Group
locations: University of Washington Internship description e internship is under Professor Shih-Chieh Hsu, who mentors PhD and post-grad students in applications of machine learning in particle physics research. Interns at the EPE ML Group will first study the basics of python programming and machine learning through lab projects. Once you learn the foundational concepts, you can begin working with other PhD/post-grad students on their research projects. Interns typically meet once per ek to present to each other about progress on their projects. Unique experiences Combining particle physics knowledge with machine learning within the world of academia Learning about the newest research in particle physics through discusison with PhD and post-grad students Self-paced and self-driven projects surprised you most? The multitude of ways in which machine learning can be applied to particle physics The lab projects make it very easy to understand the underlying concepts even for interns like me who came in with little to no experience in machine learning and particle physics. is your greatest takeaway(s) from the intemship experience? Machine learning is applicable to many aspects of this world. The concepts I learned throughout this internship aren't just limited to particle physics; they can be translated to apply to a broad spectrum of ubjects in my future computer science studies.

organization: Elementary Particle Experiment Machine Learning Team (EPE-ML)
summary: The EPE-ML team applies machine learning techniques to known problems in particle physics, using data from the Large Hadron Collider (LHC) at CERN. After initial training, I was able to take ownership of two research projects, along with a team of a few other undergraduates. We had set times for biweekly status reports to Professor Hsu and our supervising postdoc, but other times for collaborative efforts between team members were highly flexible.

locations: UW Seattle Internship description Medical imaging in its current form is expensive, inefficient, and inaccessible. We have worked on a methodology to stitch images from the OpenMV H7 camera such that in-depth LSCI imaging can be overlaid with the area of the body being captured. This will enable more efficient imaging of subcutaneous venous structures and injuries, providing a hand-held, quick alternative to traditional medical imaging. We focus on the panoramic stitching of the images captured by our handheld camera, or putting segments of separately captured images together to accurately capture a larger area. Namely, we've focused on the preservation of sufficient detail and accuracy in the final image so that it may be useful for medical applications. Unique experiences Working through unknown code and writing documentation Learning about ML applications in image processing Creating and tinkering with algorithms and workflows for tangible outcomes

organization: EMiT Lab at the University of Washington
locations: Seattle Internship description EMiT Lab is an interdisciplinary research lab focused on developing photonic technology for various future applications in engineering, medicine, and more. I focused on researching analog circuit generation code through the python repository MAGICAL, and improving documentation, as well as code to make MAGICAL ada ptable and widely accessible. We generated circuits for testing code and delved into fundamentals of circuit design alongside python and C++. My mentor was very accommodating of our schedules and helpful through our learning journey. Unique experiences Learning the importance and various aspects of circuit design, exposure to electrical engineering Enhancing skills in Python and C++ as well as high level computing Visualizing the future of analog circuit generation with mentors and professionals

locations: University of Washington Internship description e Elementary Particle Experiment Machine Learning Study Group is lead by Professor Shih-Chieh Hsu and is composed of PhD and graduate students, to use machine learning in particle physics research. These topics include anomaly detection, quick calorimetry data generation, and gravitational wave detection. Interns initially go through a basic machine learning course at UW, and later shadow a grad student mentor on their research topic. Personally, I investigated calorimetry data generation and jet tagging, comparing different models to complete each task. Unique experiences Learn about different current research topics and directions in particle physics, and how machine learning is impacting each topic. You're able to interact with graduate students and talk to them about their research. The speed of which you progress through learning your topic is very much up to you! Lots of the work is quite accessible / your own pace and can be done asynchronously. surprised you most? Using machine learning in application is quite accessible, even without any prior knowledge on the subject or of programming. There is also no need for a strong understanding of particle physics going into the internship either. The most important thing needed was the commitment to consistently spend time on the research. is your greatest takeaway(s) from the intemship experience? One of the most important things to do is communicate often with the people you are working with and make yourself an environment where you know what you should be doing. If a task is too difficult for you and ou don't know what to do, tell that to the person you are working with, because they likely do not know at.

locations: Remote Internship description The Sports Institute is dedicated to expanding participation and safety in sports and health. I conducted literature reviews, researched the symptoms of concussions and other head injuries in youth sports, as well as how to take action in the case of a concussion, and the influence of types of physical activity on patients with dementia and amnesia. Unique experiences • Contributed to the research required for the mobile application the Sports Institute is developing, ExerciseRX, which aims to mobilize healthcare. • Ability to work with industry and academic experts in the research pertaining to concussions and health injuries. • Contributed to team discussions on what kinds of exercises are most beneficial for elderly patients, particularly ones with dementia or amnesia.

summary: The UW Autism Center is devoted to supporting individuals and families affected by autism spectrum disorders through exceptional clinical services, innovative scientific research and high-quality training. locations: Online/Bothell Internship description Dan Berger is an Associate Dean for Faculty Development and Scholarship. His research focuses on the history of incarceration in Washington and more broadly the US. Dr. Berger was flexible with the internship and gave me multiple projects to work on (e.g., transcription; archival reading, and readings). I spent most of my time exploring my personal interests in history and critical theory with biweekly conversations with Dr. Berger. Unique experiences Insight into academic work e.g., the day-to-day work, journal article v. book publishing and peer review, the job market. • One-on-one conversations about critical theory e.g., abolition, Afro-pessimism, black Marxism, critical fabulation, genealogy, etc. Flexibility to explore personal research interests. at surprised you most? Dr: Berger's last book and upcoming project are both written for the public (as opposed to niche academic audiences). I was surprised to learn the difference in scale between academic publishing and commercial publishing, as well as how academic work could be directed toward the commercial press. at is your greatest takeaway(s) from the internship experience? e importance of reading and taking notes. Notes don't have to be perfectly organized nor compiled in one place but writing physical notes really does increase retention and comprehension of readings.

summary: Brunton Lab at UW-Seattle researches applications of machine learning for dynamics and control, namely fluid dynamics. The role of the research intern is primarily self-driven. The intern is expected to self-study from textbooks, read papers, and learn new technologies as fit for the completion of their projects.

summary: The EMiT Lab at UW develops large-scale and energy-efficient integrated systems with several applications in fields like life sciences, imaging, medicine, etc. During my time at EMIT, I focused on consolidating and developing code for a seamless panoramic image stitch. Our end goal was to seamlessly stitch TIFF images from the LSCI (Laser Speckle Contrast Imaging) algorithm. Interning at EMIT truly helped me improve my research and collaboration skills, and also bettered mytime management. I loved the flexibility...
activities: • Researching different methods to approach LSCI algorithms • Documenting code and presenting progress to the lab itself • Developing code to seamlessly image-stitch you the most? I was surprised by the independence that comes with research positions. It was up to me on when, where, and haw I would accomplish my goals, and also which direction my research was going in.

organization: University of Washington Mechanical Engineering Department
locations: UW MEB/Remote Internship description Professor Vashisth's lab conducts interdisciplinary research on various subjects pertaining to mechanics and material science. The lab has four core areas of research: sustainable advance composites, bio-inspired composites, atomistic simulations, and rapid manufacturing, which is the one I worked in. The project's main ocus was finding different, more efficient ways to use radio frequency heating to form composites out of carbonaceous materials and manipulate their properties. I primarily worked with a senior ME undergrad tudent and spent my time investigating research papers + learning to devise lab-setups, modeling + printing ools for the lab, and shadowing. Unique experiences Learning to model with CAD software (Onshape) + access to 3D printers Shadowing in the lab, learning to create experimental setups, experience with lab practices Sharing and discussing personal research

summary: Over the course of my internship, I had the opportunity to work on a couple different projects, primarily coding in Matlab. The lab and mentor I worked with were interested in the role of certain brain pathways in drug addiction using mouse and rat models. One of my major projects was refining and writing a program to analyze RNAscope (microscope images) to identify overlap between markers for brain activity. I also worked on a project that reconstructed microglia image stacks in 3D.
activities: One of the most interesting projects I got to contribute to was a Matlab program called DeepSqueak. My mentor created Deepsqueak, a program that denoised and classified rat calls for use by other scientists in their experiments. I spent some time training the networks in the program to improve their automatic classification. I thought it was a really unique experience to be able to work on an actively developing...

locations: CENPA new physics lab, I.JW, Seattle Internship description International particle physics research! Working with PhD and postdoc scientist mentors from around the world, you learn and code physics simulations to directly contribute to an internationally recognized particle physics experiment named PIONEER—-a pian decay experiment that tests for new physics beyond the Standard Model. This ranges from coding C++ particle collision simulations, to making Bash scripts on Linux computers, to performing Python data analysis and visualization. If you're good, you get to present your results to award-winning professors at the CENPA lab; you might even get to write a paper! Unique experiences • Generated, graphed, and analyzed terabytes of physics data to fix major s•mulation errors Installed an RTX 3070 GPIJ and then programmed it to parallelize optical photon simulation • Celebrated e successful physics presentation to Professor Hertzog with cake and donuts

organization: University of Washington/The Taskar Center for Accessible Technology
locations: Seattle Internship description The Tasker Center for Accessible Technology is a research lab with an aim of utilizing computer science to develop accessible technology, particularly for people with motor limitations or speech impairments CAT involves a selection of projects, from conducting research for King County assignments to exploring personal project topics to creating new research devices. I transitioned to a larger project under the mentorship of a UW graduate student after completing my own research project, gaining research experience at various levels. Unique experiences Learning how to incorporate Geographic Information System (GIS) applications in code. Exposure to professional research meetings and the research process. Presenting my research alongside UW undergraduates and professors.

locations: Seattle Internship description I worked on analyzing ML models for weather forecasting. I utilized existing ML models in order to conduct research regarding patterns in forecasts, how perturbations in the model affect forecasts, and more. I compared these results to other ML models to learn more about these new tools in the field of atmospheric cience. Unique experiences Attend weekly group meetings with graduate and post-doc students Work one-on-one with my mentor on an ML project related to Atmospheric Science Present my work to the group at the end of the year at surprised you most? How collaborative research is. I was not expecting the whole process to be such a team effort. Although there is a lot of individual effort required, the process involved a lot of input from my professor who helped me come up with new angles to solve problems and new ideas on tests to run. at is your greatest takeaway(s) from the intemship experience? How curious and open to new ideas everyone is in research. It seems like almost a prerequisite to conducting research. All of my research group members, as well as my mentor, were always very interested in each new result and paper. This approach to research and to science is what I will take away from my internship.

organization: UW Department of Bioethics and Humanities
locations: Seattle Internship description As a research assistant in the team under Professor Bowen, my role was to handle grants, references, and data processing specifically in the EDGE Study (Earlv Detection of GEnetic risk study). I also cooperated with the team in helping with research and literature review both in-person and remotely. Being In a relatively small research team, I was able to become very engaged in the research studies while also having a very accommodating schedule. Unique experiences Investigating the correlation between certain genes and cancer risk Researching behavioral analyses af primary health care providers Navigating REDCap, a common database web tool for research studies

organization: UW Department of Electrical and Computer Engineering
locations: Remote Internship description Dr. Anant is a professor who focuses on nanoscale materials and quantum mechanics. He is especially knowledgeable within the areas of physics and electrical engineering. He has many projects that he is currently working on that future interns may be able to help him on, although it is unlikely that one would be able to help with his new research directly, as it is very advanced. Dr. Anant did not know much about and wanted to learn more. During the second part of the year, I took that research and began compiling it into an easily readable document, which has a chance to be published at the end of the year. Overall, Dr. Anant is very smart and kind mentor who is also very accommodating to one's schedule. Unique experiences Self-Driven Research on Thermodynamics, Micro-Electronics, and Physics Writing and Potentially Publishing a Paper Explaining Heat Dissipation in Electronics Working with a UW Professor and Learning the Research Process

locations: Seattley Washington Internship description In the beginning of the internship, Professor Colin Marshall and I decided to focus on German philosopher Immanuel KantJ as that was his specialty. However, throughout the year, we explored topics such as the ethics of persuasion (namely, the art of rhetoric) as well many modern philosophers* interpretation on Kant. My meetings with Prof. Marshall revolved primarily around discussing the previous week or two weeks' readings. These readings consisted of material anywhere from chapter in a book, manuscript, or drafts. This year specifically, because of Prof. Marshall's schedule, allowed for more editorial and independent projects such as writing up commentary an his graduate students' papers and personally choosing philosophical topics to discuss. Unique experiences Flexibility to find and focus on my own philosophical interest through constant reading and research Reading and editing numerous 30+ page long drafts as part of the I<FA workshop hosted by Prof. Marshall and other academics Writing my own mini paper regarding Kant on learning from example Exploring topics such as "Punishment", "Outies", and "Ethical Theory" in a highly educative yet conversational manner

organization: UW Department of Psychiatry and Behavioral Sciences
locations: University of Washington — Seattle Internship description The New York State Behavioral Health Integration Project is running a study to look at the reach, adoption, implementation, and maintenance of Collaborative Care (CoCM) in primary care clinics in New York state. My time at the UW Department of Psychiatry and Behavioral Sciences provided me with the opportunity to complete data entry and conduct qualitative analysis of interview transcripts. Transcri ts were highlighted in sections and each one was assigned a thematic code referenced from a codebook. My responsibilities deepened as I got further into the study, and I utilized numerous skills. Unique experiences Utilizing Dedoose to code interview transcripts. Learning how to conduct qualitative analysis when I have only been introduced to quantitative analysis.

summary: I was a general research assistant for Bioethics faculty, focusing on clinical research in behavioral sciences. I helped with writing surveys, reviewing prior scientific literature, constructing bibliographies, writing grant proposals, applying detailed formatting requirements for scientific papers, and pretty much whatever else... I also sat in on research-planning meetings and helped manage logistics like contacting participants.
activities: Single-handedly drafting a survey that was later used in a clinical study and using an NIH tool to verify grant finances were probably the highlights of my internship experience!

organization: UW Stereotypes, Identity, and Belonging Lab (SIBLI
locations: University of Washington (Seattle}/Remote Internship description UWs Stereotypes, Identity, and Belonging Lab (or SIBL for short), conducts research on how people's choices and behaviors are affected bv various cultural factors. During my time there, I was able to gain hands-on experience working at a psychology research lab, completing a wide variety of tasks including data cleaning, previewing studies and surveys, proofreading papers, and much more. SIBL was very accommodative of schedule changes that I had and constantly took my feedback to improve not only their research but also their role as my employer. Unique experiences Opportunity to work on real, professional research in the field of psychology Learning new skills such as how to use analysis programs like SPSS and improving skills on other tools such as Excel He p conduct research which can make a difference!

organization: UW-Bothell Gravitational Wave Astronomy (GWA) Research Group
locations: UW-Bothell (meeting area can vary) + remote (independent or Zoom) Internship description IJW-Bothell's GWA Research Group covers several research endeavors of GWA, the main three research collaborations including LIGO (Laser Interferometer Gravitational-Wave Observatory), LISA (Laser Interferometer Space Antenna), & NANOGrav (North American Nanohertz Observatory for Gravitational Waves). I worked on reading and taking notes on several recommended sources, learning to start working through physics worksheets involving gravitational wave concepts, how online gravitational wave simulations work and what observations can be made from real data/grading pulsar plots, and more. There is opportunity to connect with a lot of college students and profs, plus access to Discord, Slack, etc, where you can choose different GWA divisions to involve yourself in, as well as opportunities to visit different GWA labs, attend conferences if interested, etc. Very self-paced/self-guided too and many resources offered. Unique experiences Hands-on data analysis — using special software/techniques to analyze direct data from observatories Collaborative research projects — opportunity to assist college students/profs with specific research and even contribute some of your ideas in their papers Technical skill development — developing technical skills in programming and simulation — Python, MATLAB, etc.

Swedish Medical Center, Seattle Internship description COPE Health Scholars is a division of COPE Health Solutions that provides educational opportunities for students interested in the healthcare field and gaining hands-on experience in various clinical settings. During my internship, I primarily assisted nurses and nursing aides in providing basic patient care as a Health Scholar. Since COPE Health Scholars is a program, there is an application process that must be completed to be accepted into the program. Unique experiences Work alongside nurses and nursing assistants by helping with vital signs, ambulation, feeding, changing, repositioning, transportation, discharge, etc. Learn the differences between various hospital departments through shifting on Neuro Telemetry, Acute Rehab, Nephrology, Postpartum, and Oncology Interact directly with patients Shadow healthcare professionals such as doctors, cardiac sonographers, physical and occupational therapists to gain insight into different career pathways in healthcare Observe medical procedures and treatments including dia ysis, surgical drain removal, stitches, parenteral feeding, and cardiac ultrasounds

locations: Bellevue Internship description Equinox Business Law Group is a law firm which serves small and mid-sized businesses in Washington. Part of the main methodology behind the firm is providing "general counsel services" (GCS) to businesses that aren't big enough to have their own in-house general counsel. GCS entails advising on all kinds of legal issues companies might face, as well as consulting for legal and management strategy. My internship projects typically fell into one of three categories: research (helping lawyers investigate legal issues clients have), marketing (looking over promotional material, social media, newsletters), and operational work (administrative systems related work). Unique experiences Valuable hands-on learning about conducting legal research and legal issues that businesses face (especially small businesses and startups) Interviews and discussions with lawyers and business owners — very useful If you're considering going into law or business

locations: Online/Seattle Internship description Fair Worlds is a startup that makes VR/AR (virtual/augmented reality) experiences for companies. In the past, they've made a VR grocery store for Whole Foods, AR chip animations for AMD, videos for NVIDIA and Dell, and even VR nature experiences for the Environmental Defense Fund. I worked on both the artistic and coding side of various projects, but focused on their Space-Time Adventure Tour project, an AR tour app of Seattle. locations: 1116 108th Ave NE, Bellevue, WA 98004 Internship description KidsQuest Children's Museum is an interactive children's museum with an emphasis on hands-on learning bout science, technology, engineering, art, and math (STEAM). In addition to the usual exhibits, KidsQuest provides a variety of programs, activities, and camps for different ages and interests. As an intern, I was able o gain experience in curriculum development and in teaching in different environments, whether informally n the floor of the museum, or during weekly after-school program classes. Unique experiences Assisting with weekly after-school programs Engaging with visitors, including parents and children ages 0-10. summary: Internship work for Loopie Laundry in Seattle, WA. Worked in pre-production and post- production for a documentary about the CEO of the company. The film will follow John as he goes from bussing tables at a restaurant to founding a successful start-up. Pre- production involved research on neorealist films, brainstorming sessions, timeline creation, storyboard creation, script creation, and revision. Post-production involved the organization of digital media and the assembly/editing of footage.
activities: • Working with many hours of existing footage • Working within the constraints of the neorealist genre • Working so closely with the CEO of a start-up

locations: Online/Renton Internship description Pasado's Safe Haven is a non-profit focusing on many different aspects of animal welfare, including running an animal sanctuary and providing services to low-income pet owners. The pet services department, where I interned at, provides dog and cat spay/neuter surgeries and pet food for low-income owners. I helped answer client emails and voicemails asking for assistance or wanting to learn more about the services Pasado's provides. I also helped work on projects my mentor gave to me, such as researching other low-cost pay/neuter clinics in the area and writing manuals for the different spay/neuter clinic stations. I also went once a month to help out at the Renton spay/neuter clinic and visited the sanctuary a few times. My mentor as also very flexible about when I could complete my work. Unique experiences Working at a spay/neuter clinic, including preparing cats for surgery and post-op monitoring Learning how to use ClinicHQ Answering client emails and questions about what services Pasado's offers

Fill the small retail section and keep adoption lobby neat.

summary: The WISER Podcast is a podcast that interviews women in different fields of surgery and talks about their experiences in them. It often focuses on implicit/explicit biases against women in surgery as well as against other communities (LGBTQ, Black, International). Episodes cover topics such as pregnancy in residency, female leadership in surgery, etc. As their art/merchandise intern, I was given a lot of freedom in terms of what I wanted the episode covers to look like and collaborated on merchandise design.

locations: Online/Seattle Internship description Admon Law Firm is a litigation-based trial firm located in Downtown Seattle, specializing in torts, business disputes, securities litigation, personal injury, and defamation law. Due to the nature of this practice, Admon Law Firm is able to offer a high degree of flexibility in its internship program, allowing a window into each of the many stages of litigation and court procedure and being particularly friendly to students wth busier schedules as many of the proceedings in King County conti nue to be held, to various extents, virtual or hybrid. In my time with Admon Law Firm, I have had the privilege of drafting and revising various legal documents, from creating formal attornevs letters (e.g., warnings), to drafting documents used in several depositions, all the way up to filing a case pleading with the Washington State Court of Appeals. Unique experiences • Creating filings for use in courts at various levels, and seeing the immediate impact of those filings as opposing counsel make attempts to resolve them. • Contacting clients from various backgrounds to gain an understanding of their perspective on how a particular event unfolded. • Research case law using professional tools (Thomson Reuters Westlaw, LexisNexis), preparing summaries and developing strategies based on precedent from various sources,

summary: Bathing Beauteas is a small, local women-owned start-up based in Seattle that sells handmade tea baths, hand lettered cards, and more. In my time at LifeWire, I presented to high schools across King County, developed curriculum that is being implemented nation-wide, and helped organize events geared toward educating teenagers. I also was able to shadow and learn more about their housing and mental health services. locations: Remote Internship description PERIOD. is a global nonprofit working to eradicate period stigma and period poverty through service, ducation, and advocacy. From combating the tampon tax to distributing millions of period products to ommunities in need, PERIOD. works to end all barriers to menstrual health. As an intern at PERIOD., I onducted case studies on current menstrual equity policies, collaborated with activists and coalitions across he country, and created the HB 1273 Handbook with WAMECO to improve HB 1273, which requires period products in schools. Unique experiences Attending and participating in menstrual equity coalition meetings across the country (ex: NYC, California, Nebraska, Alabama, etc.) Speaking at BSD board meetings and with legislators/OSPl about menstrual products in schools Re-founding WAMECO (WA Menstrual Equity Coalition) to improve implementation of HB 1273

locations: anline/Redmand Internship description My internship at the Seattle Music Teachers Association (SMTA) was about social media management, and about my learning about the inner workings of a music education organization. As a young musician, I have been Involved, without realizing, in many SMTA events, such as the MTNA competition, the Simon-Fiset competition, the Music Adjudication Program (MAP), and many other. Viewing these events through an inside lens showed me how much effort goes into music education. As a future musician, it was also incredibly valuable to see how everything was connected in the small world of music. Unique experiences Post about upcoming events, like performances and outings to social media outlets Research about the history of SMTA and how events work. Attend board meetings and suggest novel ideas from a unique (young) perspective)

In my internship, I worked with my mentor and several other preventionists to mprove how communities respond to domestic violence and strive towards creating a world where all eople can live and love without fear. We've done this by advocating for legislation that impacts survivors of buse in the Washington State Legislature and Congress through tracking bills, lobbying in Olympia, and nforming and mobilizing our membership to act on important policy and budget issues. Unique experiences Researched and analyzed legislation, attended policy briefings, and contributed to advocacy strategies that allowed me to work toward systematic change. Planned and executed large-scale projects relating to the Teen Dating Violence Awareness Action Panel. Engaged with external stakeholders, such as community members, policymakers, and potential donors.

summary: AIMI Center was established with the primary mission of developing medical AI applications and conducting biomedical imaging research to advance patient health. activities: • Learning about current research around using AI to improve and save lives • Utilizing MATLAB to create quantitative graphs for research papers • Analyzing research papers and conducting thorough literature reviews

locations: Seattle Internship description ADCS is a dental practice in downtown Seattle that sees patients four days a week. Recently, they decided to switch dental software from a software called DSN to a new software called Dentrix. As such, they needed help transferring patient data from DSN to Dentrix. My job was to transfer this datar including chart notes, health histories, and tooth charts. Additionally, I was granted the opportunity to observe the doctor during various procedures. Unique experiences Valuable experience learning the anatomy of the tooth, mouth, etc. Learning how to handle valuable patient data in a safe and secure manner. Insight into the various procedures and surgeries that the doctor performs.

locations: Bellevue Internship description Bear Computer Systems is a small local business in Bellevue that specializes in gaming PC customization. They really prioritize customer satisfaction and being extremely thorough with each customer. At Bear Computer ystems they are able to give you specific PC specs given a budget to help specialize for whatever you need our computer to run (i.e. more ram, storage, better GPU, etc.). Although the shop is physically smaller, we are able to deliver about 50 PC a month and do more PC customization as opposed to repair now. We have also worked with Valve and Amazon for local gaming competitions and tournaments. Unique experiences Building a PC using specialized parts and customization PC repair diagnostic for both hardware and software issues Bi-Weekly meetings about new tips or strategies to implement

locations: Renton Internship description BEFA is a club that rents planes to Boeing Employees. BEFA provides members with the opportunity to receive flight training towards a variety of FAA ratings. Members can also use the planes for recreational purposes. During my internship I helped with maintenance on planes and assisted in other miscellaneous tasks around the office. Unique experiences Hands on experience doing basic maintenance on planes under the supervision of a qualified mechanic. This includes such things as oil changes, 50-hour inspections, completing Airworthiness Directives, changing tires, etce Discussions with pilots and aircraft engineers about aircraft systems, airport operations, and other considerations that go into aviation. Working closely with a variety of people in the aviation field.

It is built on PyTorch, and focuses on reducing labeling costs for enterprise and academic users. My contribution was in the tutorials side, where I would write models using the 01ST IL library. My experience was more about learning Al modelling and the math behind it, rather than making big changes in the frontier. Unique experiences Learn about the calculus and statistics behind Neural Networks, Active Learning, and derivative Al techniques Write models with PyTorch and with DISTIL on top of that.

locations: Online Internship description NoHLA is a health law advocacy group that advocates for healthcare accessibility end affordability. While their focus will shift according to recent legislative developments, this year was focused on contraceptive accessibil ty and the implementation of a new state insurance program called Apple Health Expansion, which expands state coverage to undocumented people. I supported their work by lobbying, research, and meeting minutes, while learning how to analyze policy. Unique experiences Traveled to Olympia to lobby for funding for Apple Health Expansion. The trip was free on my part, • with knowledgeable, welcoming, passionate, and supportive people. Conducting an independent study which evaluates the implementation of the Reproductive Parity • Act among pharmacies across Washington. The data is being used to advocate for contraceptive accessibility and affordability.

locations: University of Washington Internship description I have a human computer interaction research internship at the University of Washington. I am part of a team f researchers under Prof. Gary Hsieh. We are currently working a project that compiles various studies on a cientific phenomenon, much of the time related to a current event or viral topic. This information, combined ith information gathered when we conduct our own studies, is used to create a meta summary where all he data is summarized and laid out in an organized fashion. My internship requires some data analysis skills s well as web development skills. Unique experiences s a computer scientist who has experience with only the more technical aspects of the field, it was a change f pace for me to enter the field of human computer interaction where psychology becomes a prominent actor as well as technical skills. I found that the experimental and research process for HCl is quite omplicated and sometimes subjective which I found to be interesting.

organization: University of Washington Center for Experimental Nuclear Physics and Astrophysics
locations: University of Washington Internship description ENPA (Center for Experimental Nuclear Physics and Astrophysics) is a research facility located and ponsored by the University of Washington. As its name implies, multiple projects take place involving nuclear physics and astrophysics, such as the g-2, PIONEER experiments (subatomic particles), and ADMX (dark matter). During my time at CENPA I was part of the PIONEER project, where I was able to develop data nalysis tools from the ground up and develop tools for the particle simulation that the project utilizes. Unique experiences Many opportunities for individual creativity regarding software and what tasks to do, such as c++ simulation work and python data analysis work. Being able to build useful tools for the project which are used by the scientists. Ability to learn from and talk to knowledgeable scientists about particle physics.

organization: University of Washington Civil and Environmental Engineering Department
locations: University of Washington Seattle I nternship Description At the UW's CEE department, I have been a part ofthe PLACE(E) lab, which is led by my mentor, Or. Bethany Gordon. During lab meetings, we worked on graduate students' research projects as well as on developing a group research project for the lab. The lab focuses on advancing climate equity through place based, community centered improvements to engineering practices, specifically in Civil Engineering. My personal projects for the lab included helping with literature reviews, creating Instagram posts about research papers by our lab members, and auditing Dr. Gordon's Behavioral Design class at the IJW. Unique experiences Learning about the research process by actMely participating in it. Networking by attending department events and lectures at IJW. Being able to learn about the inner workings of academia and gain experience in a college environment before going to college.

organization: University of Washington Department of Behavioral Science and Psychiatry
locations: Virtual Internship description At the time of my internship, Professor Cadigan was working on a study developing and testing web-based interventions to reduce high-alcohol use and teach young adult who drink to deal with negative emotions more adaptive coping skills. As an intern for Professor Cadigan, I was able to assist her and the research coordinator, Rose, in launching the study. I would have weekly meetings with Professor Cadigan and Rose where I was updated on progress with the study and given tasks to complete. Unique experiences Designed online recruitment advertisements for NIH funded study Beta test participant survey assessments aeta test web-based intervention modules Conducted literature reviews Organized data from beta testing

organization: University of Washington Mechanical Engineering Department - Human Photonics Lab
locations: Seattle Internship description The Human Photonics Lab (HPL) at UW's Mechanical Engineering Department aims to create a space for aspiring engineers and scientists to pursue a research project they are passionate about and make a difference in real-world application with a hyper focus on biotechnology in health. While I was at the HPL, I was able to take part in my own project, "VAP Buster", that aims to reduce morbidity rates for patients with ventilator-associated pneumonia (VAP) in the ICIJ. Along with my own project, I was able to observe other undergrad/graduate student projects and take part in weekly seminars where lab members would present their current research projects and findings, displaying many different research pathways and interests. Unique experiences Gave a 45-minute seminar at one of the weekly HPL Group meetings regarding my "VAP Buster" project and my additional research. Shadowed lab experiments. Drafted final report for the "VAP Buster" project to submit to the National Science Foundation (NSF).

summary: Worked with Professor Dale Durran and the Deep Learning Weather Prediction group to build a tool to enable quick conversion between file-formats necessary for model training and inference, and paper publication.
activities: A uniquely free hand in identifying points of improvement and selecting directions of approach to the problem. locations: Seattle, WA Internship description University of Washington Genome Sciences focuses on genetic research, my lab specifically focusing on researching evolutionary processes by tracking single nucleotide polymorphisms {si ngle base changes) in one or several closely related species across different geographical groups. My specific research focused on several species ofyeast, namely S paradoxus and S cerevisiae, and the variations seen in wild-type mutations versus lab-grown. Unique experiences Trained in "big data" genomic computation, addressing unique challenges of data size and cleaning to preprocess whole genomes Developed tools and packages {in both R and python) to create a pipeline to extract SNPs and graphicallyvisualize them Learned hawto create publication level posters and graphics as well as organize manuscripts Learned to perform several wet lab procedures from tetrad dissection and genetic transformation to DNA extraction and PCR

organization: UW Atmospheric Sciences Department Lacatiom Seattle/Remote Internship description Have you ever looked at a cloud and thought "wow, I wonder what type of cloud that is"? Me too! In this internship, I programmed types of machine learning models with the goal of being able to correctly classify a cloud into ane af 10 classes. Among the types of algorithms I used were SVMs (Support Vector Machines), CNNs (Convolutional Neural Networks) and KNN (K-Nearest Neighbor) algorithms. I programmed in Python, using machine learning libraries like Tensorflow, Keres, and scikit-learne The internship is very open-ended; there isn't a clear solution or one "right" wayto approach the problem. Unique experiences The wherewithal to discern visually and aesthetically pleasing pictures of clouds, perchance Training cutting-edge machine learning models and tailoring them to specific problems Doing interdisciplinary research between Computer Science and Environmental Science

locations: University of Washington Health Science Building D-514, Seattle, WA 98195 Internship description The Kaeberlein Lab studies aging at the molecular level. My specific group works to identfy genes linked to Alzheimer's disease by using RNA interference gene knockdown in C. elegans worms. Learned how to use R in order to analyze data Dry lab skills include graphing worm paralysis rates, lifespan scoring, and maintaining a spreadsheet of data for tested genes. Also required to complete quarterly presentations on a related molecular biology topic. Unique experiences Learning R was quite a new and interesting experience for me. Although R starts out very basic there are data packages that the user can download and use the new feature in order to address the problems at hand.

locations: University of Washington, Seattle Internship description rving as lab assistant in UW Materials Science & Engineering laboratory. Assisting in two different research projects Armored fish nanoindentation for the development of stronger plastic materials and Radio requency waves to eradicate invasive burrowing shrimp species. Completing day-to-day tasks within the lab, including data collection and reading scientific literature. Unique experiences Being able to work in a university laboratory is a completely different experience from a high school science lassroom. I have gotten to use machines like a Profilometer and helped with the making of polymers. t surprised you most? e lab setting is very relaxed and supportive. While many work within the lab, only a few are working on the ame projects as me, yet people from different projects offer advice and suggestions all the time. Many labs in the UW engineering department share spaces, and the professors are very welcoming and collaborative. t is your greatest takeaway(s) from the intemship experience? e research process may seem daunting at first, but there are so many opportunities to get involved in a meaningful topic. The RF wave burrowing shrimp research project is helping shellfish farmers in the Puget und, which is very relevant to our community. The positive experience I had within the research setting is a big reason why I chose to study chemical engineering in college, and I look forward to joining other research projects in the future.

summary: Vogt Lab focuses on environmental sciences. As part of my internship, I worked in a group focusing on better understanding the ways we educate others regarding environmental science/issues. While not research in a traditional sense, it provided me with first-hand experience with the ways professors approach their work and offered me opportunities to emulate that. Despite working as part of a larger team, I was very well connected with the professor.
activities: • Creating a board game used to teach K-12 students environmental principles. • Designing, preparing and running an experiment comparing teaching methods. • Just being able to hang out with and talk to a professor. Professors are very interesting people, and they like sharing.

organization: IPECC (International Peer Education on Climate Change)
locations: Online Internship description IPECC creates lessons on climate change for students to teach other students. I primarily worked on developing the junior lesson on Justice & Policy, which included environmental/climate justice, redlining, and stakeholders in climate policies. I also attended IPECC's core team meetings to help with various tasks like social media, creating graphics, editing previous lessons, and helping peer educators teach their lessons.
